commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers must approve the fail-closed capacity policy and assess live operational proof; no concrete mechanical defect currently supports an automated repair.; Cleared: The diff reuses established state-app token and checkout patterns, adds no external dependency, and does not broaden declared GitHub permissions. (sha=fe01297bfd5d04ccffcd786d659034b2d15c7e6d)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a state-repository-backed weighted provider lease, renewal loop, timeout statuses, and focused tests to gate exact-review Codex invocations.

Reproducibility: yes. at the source and simulation level: independent workflow instances currently lack provider-wide capacity coordination, and the branch models their race with separate git checkouts. Real production contention and recovery timing have not yet been reproduced in live Actions.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers must approve the fail-closed capacity policy and assess live operational proof; no concrete mechanical defect currently supports an automated repair.

target.title: fix(review): gate exact reviews by provider lease
target.body:
## Why
ClawSweeper exact reviews can run from many GitHub workflow instances at once. Worker sizing only controls local/shard concurrency; it does not protect the shared Codex provider/API-key capacity across independent runs. The important behavior change in this PR is to move overload handling from "start Codex, hit provider capacity, then retry/fail" to "reserve a global provider slot before starting Codex."

That gives us a controlled queue/backpressure point inside ClawSweeper: while global provider capacity is full, an exact review waits for a lease and reports `Waiting for Codex capacity` instead of stampeding the provider and amplifying transient transport/capacity failures. If capacity remains full until the configured wait deadline, the workflow now writes a terminal `Capacity timeout` command status instead of leaving the command looking like it is still waiting. If the provider-lease state update itself fails, the run fails explicitly rather than pretending capacity is merely full.

The lease is now renewed while the exact review is running. This matters because review runtime is not bounded by the original lease TTL in practice. A long-running review should keep its slot; if renewal stops or the lease disappears, the review step fails closed instead of continuing after its slot has expired.

## Summary
- add a state-repo-backed Codex provider lease tool with weighted capacity, TTL pruning, and acquire/release/renew commands
- gate exact event reviews on a global provider lease before invoking Codex, renew while Codex is running, then release before publishing state
- fail the review step if lease renewal stops before the review completes, avoiding silent overbooking after TTL expiry
- make current workflow capacity configuration authoritative over persisted lease state so emergency capacity changes take effect
- distinguish provider capacity waits, terminal capacity timeouts, and provider-lease state update failures in command status

## Testing
- `pnpm run build:repair`
- `node --test test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts`
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "provider lease|exact event reviews" test/clawsweeper.test.ts`
- `pnpm run check` on Node v24.15.0
- earlier on this branch: `pnpm run format:check`, `node --test --test-name-pattern "event re-review status|provider capacity|provider lease" test/clawsweeper.test.ts`, and `autoreview --mode local` clean after fixes

## Automated proof
The provider lease proof is intentionally CI-safe: it does not call Codex and does not touch production `openclaw/clawsweeper-state`. It uses a temporary local bare git repository as the shared state remote, then clones it into multiple independent checkouts to model multiple GitHub Actions workflow instances racing on the same `state` branch.

Code locations on head `fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe`:
- lease renewal document logic: [`src/repair/provider-lease.ts#L205-L225`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/src/repair/provider-lease.ts#L205-L225)
- shared-state renew command and CLI failure path: [`src/repair/provider-lease.ts#L282-L323`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/src/repair/provider-lease.ts#L282-L323)
- workflow renewal loop and fail-closed monitor: [`.github/workflows/sweep.yml#L424-L505`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/.github/workflows/sweep.yml#L424-L505)
- pure renewal unit test: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L110-L142`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L110-L142)
- shared-state capacity, release, TTL recovery proof: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L211-L331`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L211-L331)
- shared-state renewal proof: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L333-L408`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L333-L408)
- temp bare state repo and independent checkout helpers: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L423-L445`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L423-L445)
- rejected push regression: [`test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L181-L209`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts#L181-L209)
- workflow-shape assertions for renew/fail-closed wiring: [`test/clawsweeper.test.ts#L17789-L17797`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/clawsweeper.test.ts#L17789-L17797) and [`test/clawsweeper.test.ts#L18491-L18498`](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe/test/clawsweeper.test.ts#L18491-L18498)

What the shared-state proof executes:
- creates a temporary bare git origin and seeds its `state` branch
- clones that origin into separate working copies to simulate independent workflow runners
- acquires `capacity=1` from the first checkout and verifies `acquired=true` with `activeWeight=1`
- attempts a second acquisition from another checkout and verifies `acquired=false`, `activeWeight=1`, and a `provider capacity full` reason
- releases the first lease and verifies a later acquisition can succeed
- creates an unreleased short-TTL lease, waits past TTL, then verifies the next acquisition succeeds and prunes the stale lease
- renews a running holder before its original TTL expires, waits past the original TTL, and verifies another checkout is still blocked by `provider capacity full`
- waits past the renewed TTL after renewal stops, then verifies another checkout can acquire and the stale renewed holder is pruned
- separately installs a rejecting `pre-receive` hook on the bare origin and verifies a failed shared-state push throws `shared state push failed` instead of returning a false successful acquisition

Observed local results:
- `node --test test/repair/provider-lease.test.ts` passed: 9 tests, 9 pass, including the shared-state renewal proof and rejected-push regression.
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "provider lease|exact event reviews" test/clawsweeper.test.ts` passed: 2 tests, 2 pass.
- `pnpm run check` passed locally on Node v24.15.0, including 453 unit tests, 504 repair tests, coverage checks, and format check.

GitHub CI status:
- New head `fe01297bfda7394bd6ce80d2717cc49ed30d52fe` has fresh checks queued/running after removing the local-only TODO file from the PR.
- Previous head `14eed4e9b9ce0eb0848a10e1d4a95318cadfff41` passed `pnpm check`, CodeQL, Analyze, and notify before the TODO removal; code changes are otherwise unchanged.

What this proof does not claim:
- it is not real GitHub Actions concurrency against production `openclaw/clawsweeper-state`
- it does not prove cancellation recovery timing is operationally acceptable in production; cancellation still relies on TTL recovery if the job stops before release
- it does not prove the real Codex provider/API key will never throttle; it proves ClawSweeper's shared-state gate queues before invoking Codex when the configured lease capacity is full, and that running reviews keep their slot by renewing the lease

## Live proof
- Pending: collect redacted live Actions evidence for concurrent acquisition, exhausted-capacity timeout, normal release, renewal during a long-running review, and cancellation/TTL recovery before final merge readiness.
